Name: FHE 1000 TPS Target
Claim: FHE can reach 1,000 TPS with ASIC hardware acceleration (up from current 20 TPS)
Supporting Evidence: Theoretical models suggest possible; ASIC development announced; similar acceleration achieved in ZK provers
Contradicting Evidence: No working ASIC demonstrated; timeline has slipped before; 50x improvement is ambitious
What Would Disprove: FHE performance plateaus at 100-200 TPS even with ASICs; fundamental computational limits hit
Implications If Wrong: Investment thesis for FHE is timing-dependent; delays significantly impact opportunity cost

Name: ZK Enterprise Adoption
Claim: Deutsche Bank, HSBC, Walmart enterprise ZK pilots will convert to production
Source: Research document; News reports of pilots
Supporting Evidence: Pilots confirmed; continued investment; hiring in blockchain divisions
Contradicting Evidence: No production deployment announced; enterprise blockchain history (We.trade, Marco Polo failures)
What Would Disprove: Major institution publicly abandons ZK pilot; no production deployment by 2026
Confidence Level: Medium - Pilots real but conversion uncertain
Implications If Wrong: ZK market projections ($90B by 2031) may be significantly overstated

Name: Buenos Aires Identity Replicability
Claim: Buenos Aires ZK identity deployment (3.6M citizens) is replicable model for other cities/nations
Supporting Evidence: Working deployment; positive user feedback reported; technical documentation available
Contradicting Evidence: Unique political circumstances; specific vendor relationship; no other major deployments announced
What Would Disprove: Multiple attempted replications fail; Buenos Aires encounters scaling issues; political reversal
Implications If Wrong: DID investment thesis depends on government adoption path; may need to focus on private sector

Name: RWA $13.55T by 2030
Claim: Real-world asset tokenization market reaches $3.5-30T (median $13.55T) by 2030
Supporting Evidence: BlackRock BUIDL growth ($2.9B); regulatory momentum; institutional interest
Contradicting Evidence: Secondary market liquidity not forming; regulatory clarity lacking; custody solutions immature
What Would Disprove: RWA market stalls below $500B by 2028; major institutional player exits
Implications If Wrong: Sector prioritization may need revision; focus on infrastructure vs direct RWA plays

Name: ZK L2 60.7% CAGR
Claim: ZK L2 solutions market grows at 60.7% CAGR to reach $90B by 2031
Supporting Evidence: Strong current growth; major funding; developer activity; institutional interest
Contradicting Evidence: Optimistic rollups still dominate (83-85%); ZK complexity slowing adoption
What Would Disprove: ZK rollups fail to capture majority share by 2028; technology complexity proves insurmountable
Confidence Level: Medium - Growth real but magnitude uncertain
Implications If Wrong: May overweight ZK vs optimistic rollups in portfolio; adjust position sizing

Name: QV Improves Participation
Claim: Quadratic voting adoption (100+ DAOs) correlates with improved participation vs baseline 17%
Supporting Evidence: Theoretical basis strong; some positive case studies
Contradicting Evidence: No systematic comparison published; self-selection bias (DAOs choosing QV may differ)
What Would Disprove: Systematic study shows no participation improvement; QV DAOs show similar 17% rates
Implications If Wrong: Quadratic funding infrastructure thesis weakened; may be solving wrong problem

Name: Chainlink 46-58% Market Share
Claim: Chainlink maintains 46-58% of oracle market share
Supporting Evidence: Multiple data sources confirm; TVS metrics available; integration counts verifiable
Contradicting Evidence: Measurement methodology varies; some protocols not counted
Implications If Wrong: Oracle investment thesis about competition may need revision if share actually higher/lower

Name: $34B DAO Treasury Holdings
Claim: Total DAO treasuries hold approximately $34 billion
Contradicting Evidence: Counting methodology varies; some treasuries multi-counted
What Would Disprove: Independent verification shows significantly different number (>50% variance)
Implications If Wrong: DAO governance thesis scale assumptions may need adjustment

Name: 80% Compliance Cost Reduction (EAS)
Claim: Financial institutions report 80% reduction in compliance costs with attestation systems
Supporting Evidence: Case studies cited; logical mechanism (automation reduces manual process)
Contradicting Evidence: Vendor incentive to overstate; no independent verification; small sample size
What Would Disprove: Independent audit shows <30% improvement; adoption stalls despite claimed benefits
Implications If Wrong: DID/Attestation investment thesis may overstate near-term value proposition

Name: Perp DEX 872% YoY Growth
Claim: Perpetual DEX volume grew 872% year-over-year in 2024 to $1.4T+
Supporting Evidence: On-chain volume verifiable; multiple sources confirm; Hyperliquid growth documented
Contradicting Evidence: Single year may be anomaly; 2023 was depressed baseline
What Would Disprove: Volume contracts significantly in 2025; growth was one-time shift from CeFi
Implications If Wrong: If growth doesn't sustain, derivatives thesis needs revision

Name: Sequencer Decentralization Timelines
Claim: Major L2s will decentralize sequencers per their roadmaps
Supporting Evidence: Roadmaps published; some technical progress (shared sequencer research)
Contradicting Evidence: Timeline already slipped multiple times; revenue model conflict; no concrete dates
What Would Disprove: No major L2 achieves decentralized sequencer by end of 2026
Implications If Wrong: Rollup d/acc alignment may be overstated; sequencer risk underweighted

Name: Stablecoin $27.6T Volume
Claim: Annual on-chain stablecoin volume is $27.6T (exceeding Visa+Mastercard)
Supporting Evidence: On-chain verifiable; multiple data providers confirm
Contradicting Evidence: Wash trading inflates numbers; some volume is MEV/arbitrage not 'real' transactions
What Would Disprove: Methodology adjustment shows <$10T genuine transaction volume
Confidence Level: High with caveats - Measurable but definitional issues
Implications If Wrong: Stablecoin market size may be overstated; focus on genuine payment use case

Name: Safe $100B+ Secured
Claim: Safe (Gnosis Safe) secures $100B+ in assets across 7M+ accounts
Supporting Evidence: On-chain verifiable; Safe tracks publicly; widely cited
Contradicting Evidence: Peak metrics may be used; current may be lower
What Would Disprove: Independent verification shows significantly lower TVL

Name: MediLedger DSCSA Compliance
Claim: MediLedger consortium (24+ companies) achieved DSCSA compliance with sub-second verification
Supporting Evidence: FDA participation confirmed; major pharma companies listed; regulatory requirement driving adoption
Contradicting Evidence: Consortium membership doesn't equal active usage; may be compliance checkbox
What Would Disprove: Major participant exits; DSCSA enforcement delayed or changed
Implications If Wrong: Supply chain thesis validation; strongest enterprise blockchain case

Name: Gitcoin Passport 2M+ Users
Claim: Gitcoin Passport has 2M+ users and 34M+ credentials
Contradicting Evidence: Active users vs total signups may differ; credential count methodology unclear
What Would Disprove: Independent verification shows significantly lower active usage

Name: Estonia 50%+ Online Voting
Claim: Estonia achieved >50% of votes cast online in 2023 (first globally for legally binding elections)
Supporting Evidence: Estonian government statistics; international election observer reports
Contradicting Evidence: Unique circumstances (small population, digital-native government, decades of investment)
What Would Disprove: System compromise discovered; major security incident; adoption reverses
Implications If Wrong: Governance thesis validation; but replicability uncertain
